none.justification: Badly incomplete: it correctly classifies the few sites it lists, including debug.c jemalloc mallctl as allocator-related, but misses most USE_JEMALLOC files/sites and the Makefile jemalloc build/link effect; it also undercounts HAVE_BACKTRACE versus the GT.

ccodegraph.justification: Mostly correct on the USE_JEMALLOC inventory and subsystem classification across the expected 13 source files, including debug.c as allocator-related. Real gaps remain: it omits the GT-required Makefile build-system effect and undercounts/misstates HAVE_BACKTRACE occurrences.

codegraph.justification: Mostly correct: it recovers the broader #if defined(USE_JEMALLOC) set, classifies all as memory allocator, and at least mentions the Makefile jemalloc control. However, it frames many sites as an addendum rather than the main answer and undercounts HAVE_BACKTRACE, missing the GT's full 15 occurrences.

cbm.justification: Badly incomplete for the same core reason as Answer A: it only reports the four strict #ifdef USE_JEMALLOC sites in debug.c/zmalloc.c, missing the many #if defined USE_JEMALLOC sites and the Makefile effect. Its HAVE_BACKTRACE classification is directionally right but undercounts the GT occurrence total.
